formulation_candidates.ambiguity_note: This represents the PLGA-ITZ-NS formulation after optimization of poloxamer 188 concentration (3 mg/mL) and PLGA:ITZ ratio (10:1).
This is the optimal PLGA-ITZ-NS formulation (FC1) after being freeze-dried with 2% sucrose, selected for pharmacokinetic studies.
Commercial formulation used as a comparator in pharmacokinetic studies.

relation_hints.note: FC2 is a freeze-dried variant of the optimal PLGA-ITZ-NS (FC1).
FC2 (PLGA-ITZ-NS) was compared to Sporanox® (FC3) in pharmacokinetic studies.

evidence_spans.source_locator_text: PLGA-ITZ-NS were prepared by a nanoprecipitation method and optimized by modifying the surfactant poloxamer 188 concentration and PLGA:ITZ ratio.
evidence_spans.supporting_text: PLGA-ITZ-NS were prepared by a nanoprecipitation method and optimized by modifying the surfactant poloxamer 188 concentration and PLGA:ITZ ratio.

evidence_spans.source_locator_text: Optimal PLGA-ITZ-NS had a mean particle size of about 200 nm with a high homogeneity (polydispersity index ≈0.2), favorable zeta potential (approximately −20 to −30 mV) and encapsulation efficiency (72%).
evidence_spans.supporting_text: Optimal PLGA-ITZ-NS had a mean particle size of about 200 nm with a high homogeneity (polydispersity index ≈0.2), favorable zeta potential (approximately −20 to −30 mV) and encapsulation efficiency (72%).

evidence_spans.source_locator_text: In addition, 2% w/v sucrose was selected as a lyoprotectant for NS freeze-drying.
evidence_spans.supporting_text: In addition, 2% w/v sucrose was selected as a lyoprotectant for NS freeze-drying.

evidence_spans.source_locator_text: The unloaded PLGA nanoparticles and the PLGA-ITZ-NS were synthesized by a nanoprecipitation method.16 In addition, a 1% (w/v) PLGA solution was prepared by dissolving the PLGA (50:50) in acetone. To get an optimal concentration of the surfactant used to prepare PLGA nanoparticles, 5 mL of PLGA (1% w/v) solution was slowly added to four aqueous solutions (50 mL of each) containing 2.5, 3, 4, and 10 mg/mL of nonionic surfactant (poloxamer 188) with stirring.
evidence_spans.supporting_text: The unloaded PLGA nanoparticles and the PLGA-ITZ-NS were synthesized by a nanoprecipitation method.16 In addition, a 1% (w/v) PLGA solution was prepared by dissolving the PLGA (50:50) in acetone. To get an optimal concentration of the surfactant used to prepare PLGA nanoparticles, 5 mL of PLGA (1% w/v) solution was slowly added to four aqueous solutions (50 mL of each) containing 2.5, 3, 4, and 10 mg/mL of nonionic surfactant (poloxamer 188) with stirring.

evidence_spans.source_locator_text: After the optimal surfactant concentration had been determined, various amounts of ITZ were dissolved into three solutions (5 mL of each) of 1% (w/v) PLGA in acetone to obtain PLGA:ITZ (w/w) ratios of 5:1, 10:1, and 15:1, respectively. The organic phase was added at a constant rate of 0.3 mL per minute with stirring to 50 mL of the aqueous phase, containing an optimal concentration of poloxamer 188.
evidence_spans.supporting_text: After the optimal surfactant concentration had been determined, various amounts of ITZ were dissolved into three solutions (5 mL of each) of 1% (w/v) PLGA in acetone to obtain PLGA:ITZ (w/w) ratios of 5:1, 10:1, and 15:1, respectively. The organic phase was added at a constant rate of 0.3 mL per minute with stirring to 50 mL of the aqueous phase, containing an optimal concentration of poloxamer 188.

evidence_spans.source_locator_text: To discover the best lyoprotectant and optimal concentration for lyophilization, the PLGA-ITZ-NS were freeze-dried in the presence of sucrose, mannitol, dextrose, or HP-β-CD at 2, 5, or 10% w/v.
evidence_spans.supporting_text: To discover the best lyoprotectant and optimal concentration for lyophilization, the PLGA-ITZ-NS were freeze-dried in the presence of sucrose, mannitol, dextrose, or HP-β-CD at 2, 5, or 10% w/v.

evidence_spans.source_locator_text: When the poloxamer 188 concentration was 3 mg/mL, the optimal PLGA-ITZ-NS formulation was obtained with the smallest particle size (189±5 nm) and PDI (0.08±0.02). Therefore, 3 mg/mL of poloxamer 188 was chosen for the preparation of PLGA-ITZ-NS during the whole study.
evidence_spans.supporting_text: When the poloxamer 188 concentration was 3 mg/mL, the optimal PLGA-ITZ-NS formulation was obtained with the smallest particle size (189±5 nm) and PDI (0.08±0.02). Therefore, 3 mg/mL of poloxamer 188 was chosen for the preparation of PLGA-ITZ-NS during the whole study.

evidence_spans.source_locator_text: Sporanox® (Janssen Pharmaceutica NV, Beerse, Belgium) was the only commercial intravenous formulation of ITZ, which was solubilized by 40% hydroxypropyl-beta-cyclodextrin (HP-β-CD).
evidence_spans.supporting_text: Sporanox® (Janssen Pharmaceutica NV, Beerse, Belgium) was the only commercial intravenous formulation of ITZ, which was solubilized by 40% hydroxypropyl-beta-cyclodextrin (HP-β-CD).

evidence_spans.source_locator_text: Another group of rats (n=4) was administered with a 10 mg/kg dose of Sporanox® (approximately 1 mL at 3.333 mg/mL) at the same condition.
evidence_spans.supporting_text: Another group of rats (n=4) was administered with a 10 mg/kg dose of Sporanox® (approximately 1 mL at 3.333 mg/mL) at the same condition.

evidence_spans.source_locator_text: Our newly developed PLGA-ITZ-NS formulation without HP-β-CD has shown a great sustained release profile with a low-loading dose. Thus, we conclude this nanoformulation appears to be a promising injectable ITZ formulation for future clinical application.
evidence_spans.supporting_text: Our newly developed PLGA-ITZ-NS formulation without HP-β-CD has shown a great sustained release profile with a low-loading dose. Thus, we conclude this nanoformulation appears to be a promising injectable ITZ formulation for future clinical application.

evidence_spans.source_locator_text: The morphology of lyophilized PLGA-ITZ-NS was exhibited by SEM (Figure 1A). It was evident that the particles had spherical shape with a smooth surface. It should also be noted that these particles were fairly uniform in size, and the average particle size was consistent with what was obtained by DLS (Figure 1B).
evidence_spans.supporting_text: The morphology of lyophilized PLGA-ITZ-NS was exhibited by SEM (Figure 1A). It was evident that the particles had spherical shape with a smooth surface. It should also be noted that these particles were fairly uniform in size, and the average particle size was consistent with what was obtained by DLS (Figure 1B).

evidence_spans.supporting_text: Figure 1 (A) SEM image and (B) DLS size distribution of PLGA-ITZ-NS.

evidence_spans.supporting_text: Figure 3 shows the plasma concentration versus time profiles of ITZ after the intravenous administration of our optimal PLGA-ITZ-NS formulation and Sporanox® (control formulation).

evidence_spans.supporting_text: Table 4 Dose-normalized pharmacokinetics parameters of ITZ in plasma after a single intravenous administration of PLGA-ITZ-NS at 5 mg/mL to rats (n=5) or Sporanox® at 10 mg/mL to rats (n=4)

evidence_spans.source_locator_text: The relevant pharmacokinetic parameters are summarized in Table 4. There are no significant differences on the dose-normalized area under the plasma drug concentration–time curve and clearance between PLGA-ITZ-NS and Sporanox®, suggesting a bioequivalence of the two formulations.
evidence_spans.supporting_text: The relevant pharmacokinetic parameters are summarized in Table 4. There are no significant differences on the dose-normalized area under the plasma drug concentration–time curve and clearance between PLGA-ITZ-NS and Sporanox®, suggesting a bioequivalence of the two formulations.

evidence_spans.source_locator_text: PLGA (polylactic-glycolic acid, ratio: 50:50, molecular weight 30,000–60,000 Da), poloxamer 188 (Pluronic® F-68), ITZ, Dulbecco’s phosphate buffered saline (Life Technologies, Carlsbad, CA, USA), dextrose, sucrose, mannitol, acetone, acetonitrile, and HP-β-CD were all purchased from Sigma-Aldrich (St Louis, MO, USA).
evidence_spans.supporting_text: PLGA (polylactic-glycolic acid, ratio: 50:50, molecular weight 30,000–60,000 Da), poloxamer 188 (Pluronic® F-68), ITZ, Dulbecco’s phosphate buffered saline (Life Technologies, Carlsbad, CA, USA), dextrose, sucrose, mannitol, acetone, acetonitrile, and HP-β-CD were all purchased from Sigma-Aldrich (St Louis, MO, USA).

evidence_spans.source_locator_text: Sporanox® was purchased from Walgreens (Deerfield, IL, USA).
evidence_spans.supporting_text: Sporanox® was purchased from Walgreens (Deerfield, IL, USA).

evidence_spans.source_locator_text: In addition, a 1% (w/v) PLGA solution was prepared by dissolving the PLGA (50:50) in acetone.
evidence_spans.supporting_text: In addition, a 1% (w/v) PLGA solution was prepared by dissolving the PLGA (50:50) in acetone.

evidence_spans.source_locator_text: To obtain the percentage of ITZ encapsulated in PLGA NS, 30 mL of the PLGA-ITZ-NS suspension in the “Preparation of PLGA-ITZ-NS” section was filtered and centrifuged at 8,000 rpm for 6 minutes. After removing the supernatant, acetonitrile was added to the pellets, followed by sonication for 15 minutes to break the polymer network and release the ITZ. The amount of ITZ was analyzed by the high performance liquid chromatography (HPLC) assay developed by Darouiche et al.17 The encapsulation efficiency (EE) was calculated by the following equation: Encapsulation efficiency % = Amount of drug in nanosphere / Amount of drug used in wholeformulation process × 100%
evidence_spans.supporting_text: To obtain the percentage of ITZ encapsulated in PLGA NS, 30 mL of the PLGA-ITZ-NS suspension in the “Preparation of PLGA-ITZ-NS” section was filtered and centrifuged at 8,000 rpm for 6 minutes. After removing the supernatant, acetonitrile was added to the pellets, followed by sonication for 15 minutes to break the polymer network and release the ITZ. The amount of ITZ was analyzed by the high performance liquid chromatography (HPLC) assay developed by Darouiche et al.17 The encapsulation efficiency (EE) was calculated by the following equation: Encapsulation efficiency % = Amount of drug in nanosphere / Amount of drug used in wholeformulation process × 100%

evidence_spans.source_locator_text: The particle size, size distribution polydispersity index (PDI), and the zeta potential were measured by dynamic light scattering (DLS) method, using the Zetasizer Nano ZS (Malvern Instruments, Malvern, UK). Each analysis was performed in triplicate.
evidence_spans.supporting_text: The particle size, size distribution polydispersity index (PDI), and the zeta potential were measured by dynamic light scattering (DLS) method, using the Zetasizer Nano ZS (Malvern Instruments, Malvern, UK). Each analysis was performed in triplicate.

evidence_spans.source_locator_text: The external morphology of PLGA-ITZ-NS was examined by scanning electron microscopy (SEM). A lyophilized sample was affixed onto an aluminum sample holder with a conductive double-sided carbon tape, followed by imaging with the Hitachi S-4800-II SEM (Hitachi Ltd, Tokyo, Japan) to obtain the SEM images.
evidence_spans.supporting_text: The external morphology of PLGA-ITZ-NS was examined by scanning electron microscopy (SEM). A lyophilized sample was affixed onto an aluminum sample holder with a conductive double-sided carbon tape, followed by imaging with the Hitachi S-4800-II SEM (Hitachi Ltd, Tokyo, Japan) to obtain the SEM images.

evidence_spans.source_locator_text: The control sample was PLGA-ITZ-NS alone, without any lyoprotectant.
evidence_spans.supporting_text: The control sample was PLGA-ITZ-NS alone, without any lyoprotectant.

evidence_spans.source_locator_text: The samples were lyophilized under 0.018 mbar vacuum (condenser temperature of −80°C) for 24 hours in a FreeZone Freeze-Dry System (Labconco, Kansas City, MO, USA). The freeze-dried samples were reconstituted in deionized water by shaking. The resuspending time for each sample was recorded. The colloidal NS was then evaluated by the DLS method for particle size, PDI, and zeta potential analysis. The ratio of the final and initial particle size (Sf/Si) was also calculated.
evidence_spans.supporting_text: The samples were lyophilized under 0.018 mbar vacuum (condenser temperature of −80°C) for 24 hours in a FreeZone Freeze-Dry System (Labconco, Kansas City, MO, USA). The freeze-dried samples were reconstituted in deionized water by shaking. The colloidal NS was then evaluated by the DLS method for particle size, PDI, and zeta potential analysis. The ratio of the final and initial particle size (Sf/Si) was also calculated.

evidence_spans.source_locator_text: One group of rats (n=5) received 5 mg/kg dose of PLGA-ITZ-NS (approximately 1.7 mL at 0.985 mg/mL) through an intravenous infusion using a 23-gauge needle.
evidence_spans.supporting_text: One group of rats (n=5) received 5 mg/kg dose of PLGA-ITZ-NS (approximately 1.7 mL at 0.985 mg/mL) through an intravenous infusion using a 23-gauge needle.

evidence_spans.source_locator_text: Optimal PLGA-ITZ-NS had a mean particle size of about 200 nm with a high homogeneity (polydispersity index ≈0.2), favorable zeta potential (approximately −20 to −30 mV) and encapsulation efficiency (72%).
evidence_spans.supporting_text: Optimal PLGA-ITZ-NS had a mean particle size of about 200 nm with a high homogeneity (polydispersity index ≈0.2), favorable zeta potential (approximately −20 to −30 mV) and encapsulation efficiency (72%).

evidence_spans.source_locator_text: In addition, 2% w/v sucrose was selected as a lyoprotectant for NS freeze-drying.
evidence_spans.supporting_text: In addition, 2% w/v sucrose was selected as a lyoprotectant for NS freeze-drying.

evidence_spans.source_locator_text: The in vivo study showed that the NS formulation has a similar systemic bioavailability to Sporanox® while providing a sustained plasma level (.100 ng/mL) for up to 24 hours after intravenous administration.
evidence_spans.supporting_text: The in vivo study showed that the NS formulation has a similar systemic bioavailability to Sporanox® while providing a sustained plasma level (.100 ng/mL) for up to 24 hours after intravenous administration.

evidence_spans.source_locator_text: Our newly developed PLGA-ITZ-NS has shown great sustained release and comparable bioavailability with Sporanox®, therefore having the potential to be an alternative injectable formulation of ITZ.
evidence_spans.supporting_text: Our newly developed PLGA-ITZ-NS has shown great sustained release and comparable bioavailability with Sporanox®, therefore having the potential to be an alternative injectable formulation of ITZ.

evidence_spans.source_locator_text: PLGA-ITZ-NS, with a ratio of 5:1 and 10:1, gave similar small particle sizes, but the latter had lower PDI and higher EE, which were more preferable. NS, with a ratio of 15:1, provided similar EE as NS with its ratio of 10:1, but the particle size was larger.
evidence_spans.supporting_text: PLGA-ITZ-NS, with a ratio of 5:1 and 10:1, gave similar small particle sizes, but the latter had lower PDI and higher EE, which were more preferable. NS, with a ratio of 15:1, provided similar EE as NS with its ratio of 10:1, but the particle size was larger.

evidence_spans.source_locator_text: When considering the particle size or the Sf/Si ratio, mannitol was not as good as the other three lyoprotectants. Meanwhile, NS freeze-dried with dextrose had a longer resuspending time. It seemed that 2% sucrose and 2% HP-β-CD provided the most stable, uniform, and smallest particles with the shortest resuspending time.
evidence_spans.supporting_text: When considering the particle size or the Sf/Si ratio, mannitol was not as good as the other three lyoprotectants. Meanwhile, NS freeze-dried with dextrose had a longer resuspending time. It seemed that 2% sucrose and 2% HP-β-CD provided the most stable, uniform, and smallest particles with the shortest resuspending time.

evidence_spans.source_locator_text: The control sample of PLGA-ITZ-NS that was freeze-dried without any lyoprotectant was found mostly aggregated; therefore, lyoprotectant is necessary for the lyophilization process.
evidence_spans.supporting_text: The control sample of PLGA-ITZ-NS that was freeze-dried without any lyoprotectant was found mostly aggregated; therefore, lyoprotectant is necessary for the lyophilization process.

unassigned_observations.note: Mannitol and dextrose were less effective lyoprotectants compared to sucrose and HP-β-CD based on particle size, Sf/Si ratio, and resuspending time.
HP-β-CD was avoided as a lyoprotectant due to concerns about slow clearance and renal impairment, despite providing good physicochemical properties for the freeze-dried nanospheres.

table_formulation_scopes.evidence_span: Table 1 Physicochemical properties of unloaded PLGA nanoparticles stabilized by different concentrations of poloxamer 188
Table 2 Physicochemical properties of PLGA-ITZ-NS with different PLGA:ITZ initial ratios
Table 3 Physicochemical properties of freeze-dried PLGA-ITZ-NS with various types and concentrations of lyoprotectants

selection_markers.evidence_span: When the poloxamer 188 concentration was 3 mg/mL, the optimal PLGA-ITZ-NS formulation was obtained with the smallest particle size (189±5 nm) and PDI (0.08±0.02). Therefore, 3 mg/mL of poloxamer 188 was chosen for the preparation of PLGA-ITZ-NS during the whole study.

Therefore, the PLGA-ITZ prepared with an initial ratio of 10:1 was chosen as the optimal formulation when considering particle size, PDI and EE comprehensively, and was utilized for the formulation of all the following studies.
2% sucrose instead of HP-β-CD was chosen as a lyoprotectant for freeze-drying PLGA-ITZ-NS, which was then used in the pharmacokinetics study.

inheritance_markers.evidence_span: After the optimal surfactant concentration had been determined, various amounts of ITZ were dissolved into three solutions (5 mL of each) of 1% (w/v) PLGA in acetone to obtain PLGA:ITZ (w/w) ratios of 5:1, 10:1, and 15:1, respectively. The organic phase was added at a constant rate of 0.3 mL per minute with stirring to 50 mL of the aqueous phase, containing an optimal concentration of poloxamer 188.

Therefore, the PLGA-ITZ prepared with an initial ratio of 10:1 was chosen as the optimal formulation when considering particle size, PDI and EE comprehensively, and was utilized for the formulation of all the following studies.
2% sucrose instead of HP-β-CD was chosen as a lyoprotectant for freeze-drying PLGA-ITZ-NS, which was then used in the pharmacokinetics study.
